programme.title: Barefooted, Running, And Asking For A Ride
programme.desc: The State called people who lived near the deadly parade crash to identify Darrell Brooks as the one who abandoned an SUV and ran away. Police said it was that vehicle that rammed into the crowd killing six people and injuring more than 60 others.

programme.title: Mom Calls For A Lyft Ride
programme.desc: Darrell Brooks's mother called for a ride minutes after the deadly Christmas parade crash. Brooks was accused of ramming into the crowd and killing six people. Police said he abandoned the SUV and ran away in a residential area.

programme.title: A Kind, Helpful Resident
programme.desc: Jurors heard from a man in the deadly parade crash trial who was watching football when he said Darrell Brooks rang his doorbell. He said the defendant was barefoot and cold and asking for help.

programme.title: From Neighborly Help To Arrest
programme.desc: Darrell Brooks got his turn to question the homeowner who helped him minutes after police claimed Brooks ran over people at a Christmas parade killing six people.

programme.title: Hearing From An Arresting Officer
programme.desc: Police said Darrell Brooks ran from his abandoned SUV and began knocking on doors in a neighborhood asking to use a phone to call for a ride. Jurors watched police bodycam of the arrest of Darrell Brooks.
